steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The “but” reveals the core contrast: the reasons initially appear compelling (explaining the regular characterization), yet closer inspection exposes subtleties in Thoreau's style that don't fit the movement.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
“Irrefutable” (undeniable, self-evident) fits perfectly. It presents the reasons as obviously convincing (explaining why scholars regularly make this characterization), but the second clause then exposes the hidden problem.

The other three either misrepresent what kind of reasons these are (“pretentious,” “provocative”) or anticipate the problem rather than the initial appearance (“incongruous”).

D is correct.
